title: salix alba f. vitellina
intro: Salix alba f. vitellina, also known as White Willow, is a cultivar of the white willow tree, a deciduous tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The bark of the White Willow has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which are attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Salix alba f. vitellina have been used to alleviate various health issues, including menstrual cramps, headaches, and arthritis, making it a popular herbal remedy. Salix alba f. vitellina can be prepared in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
